purpose: Validate that README artifact classes are named and bounded without promoting repository-held documents, contracts, validators, metadata packages, specifications, or substrate into current operational capability.
required_boundary_phrases: static, GitHub Pages deployed, browser-side, deterministic, scenario-driven, non-persistent public artifact
does not complete the operational evidence packet
does not claim DOI, archive, journal, or search-submission completion
Operational use remains a legitimate future threshold event

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(evidence packet completed|completed evidence packet|operational evidence exists|current operational evidence)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: An evidence contract may define future requirements, but it must not be described as completed operational evidence.

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(validators?|validation scripts?)\b[^\n.]{0,120}\b(runtime enforcement|production governance control|live compliance enforcement|operational audit system)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: Validators are repository constraint checkers, not runtime enforcement systems.

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(metadata|citation authority|publication apparatus)\b[^\n.]{0,120}\b(DOI minted|archive deposited|journal publication|institutional validation|external endorsement|search submission completed)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: Metadata packages are repository-held authority packages, not proof of external publication events.

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(unapplied migration|implementation substrate|scaffold)\b[^\n.]{0,120}\b(live database|verified RLS|verified tenant isolation|live application-data persistence|production audit ledger)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: Unapplied substrate must not be promoted into live infrastructure.

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(theoretical document|conceptual/specification document|specification)\b[^\n.]{0,120}\b(implemented system|runtime capability|deployed component|proof of execution|operational workflow execution)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: Theory and specification documents describe or frame; they do not prove built capability.
Repository-held artifacts should be described by class, not as operational system behavior.

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(operational use achieved|operational use is current|operational-use threshold met|threshold has been reached)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: Operational use is a legitimate future threshold event, not a current claim.

restricted_claim_patterns.pattern: \b(production SaaS exists|customer workspace exists|tenant isolation exists|public NEXUS runtime exists|model API execution exists|Palisade is instantiated|Weave is runnable)\b
restricted_claim_patterns.reason: README must not claim current operational capabilities that are not instantiated.
